title: Korzok Monastery
tagline: Korzok Monastery — spiritual town of Ladakh (UT)

overview.short: Korzok, དཀོར་མཛོད་ (wylie dkor-mdzod) is a Tibetan Buddhist monastery belonging to the Drukpa Lineage.
overview.description: Korzok, དཀོར་མཛོད་ (wylie dkor-mdzod) is a Tibetan Buddhist monastery belonging to the Drukpa Lineage. It is located in the Korzok village, on the northwestern bank of Tso Moriri, a lake in Leh District of Ladakh, a union territory of India. The gompa (monastery), at 4,560 metres (14,960 ft), houses a Shakyamuni Buddha and other statues. It is home to about seventy monks.

topPlaces.name: Karzok
topPlaces.description: Karzok or Korzok (at an altitude of 14,995 ft or 4,570 m to 15,075 ft or 4,595 m above sea level), on the northwestern shores of the Tso Moriri lake, is a village in the Rupshu region and a block of the Changthang district in Ladakh, India. Karzok is among the highest permanent settlements in the world.

topPlaces.name: Tso Moriri
topPlaces.description: Tso Moriri or Lake Moriri (Tibetan: ལྷ་མོའི་བླ་མཚོ, Wylie: lha mo bla mtsho, 'Mountain Lake'), 4,522 m (14,836 ft), is a high-altitude lake and a Ramsar wetland site in the remote Changthang Plateau (literally: northern plains) in the Changthang district of the union territory of Ladakh in India. As the largest high-altitude lake entirely within India and Ladakh's Trans-Himalayan region, it spans 26 km north-south and 4-8 km wide.

items.note: Karzok or Korzok (at an altitude of 14,995 ft or 4,570 m to 15,075 ft or 4,595 m above sea level), on the northwestern shores of the Tso Moriri lake, is a village in the Rupshu region and a block of the Changthang district in Ladakh, India. Karzok is among the highest permanent settlements in the world.

Tso Moriri or Lake Moriri (Tibetan: ལྷ་མོའི་བླ་མཚོ, Wylie: lha mo bla mtsho, 'Mountain Lake'), 4,522 m (14,836 ft), is a high-altitude lake and a Ramsar wetland site in the remote Changthang Plateau (literally: northern plains) in the Changthang district of the union territory of Ladakh in India. As the largest high-altitude lake entirely within India and Ladakh's Trans-Himalayan region, it spans 26 km north-south and 4-8 km wide.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Korzok Monastery?
faq.a: The best time to visit Korzok Monastery is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Korzok Monastery?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kushok Bakula Rimpochee Airport, Leh (146 km) and the nearest railway station is Chandigarh Railway Station (288 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Korzok Monastery?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Korzok Monastery at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Korzok Monastery?
faq.a: Stays in Korzok Monastery start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Korzok Monastery?
faq.a: The top attractions are Karzok, Tso Moriri.
